children.text: During the eighteen month period following promulgation of implementing rules pursuant to subsection (e), a person described in subsection (b) shall be eligible for an allotment of not more than two parcels of federal land totaling 160 acres or less under the Act of (chapter 2469; ), as such Act was in effect before .134 Stat. 197So in original. Probably should be capitalized.1May 17, 1906December 18, 1971
Allotments may be selected only from lands that were vacant, unappropriated, and unreserved on the date when the person eligible for the allotment first used and occupied those lands.

children.label: A
children.text: lands upon which a native or non-native campsite is located, except for a campsite used primarily by the person selecting the allotment;

children.label: B
children.text: lands selected by, but not conveyed to, the State of Alaska pursuant to the Alaska Statehood Act or any other provision of law;

children.label: C
children.text: lands selected by, but not conveyed to, a Village or Regional Corporation;

children.label: D
children.text: lands designated as wilderness by statute;

children.label: E
children.text: acquired lands;

children.label: F
children.text: lands containing a building, permanent structure, or other development owned or controlled by the United States, another unit of government, or a person other than the person selecting the allotment;

children.label: G
children.text: lands withdrawn or reserved for national defense purposes other than National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska;

children.label: H
children.text: National Forest Lands; and

children.label: A
children.text: lands withdrawn pursuant to which were not selected, or were relinquished after selection;section 1610(a)(1) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: lands contiguous to the outer boundary of lands withdrawn pursuant to , except lands excluded from selection by a provision of subsection (a)(3) and lands within a National Park; orsection 1610(a)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: vacant, unappropriated and unreserved lands.

After consultation with a person entitled to an allotment within a Conservation System Unit, the Secretary may convey alternative lands of equal acreage, including lands within a Conservation System Unit, to that person if the Secretary determines that the allotment would be incompatible with a purpose for which the Conservation System Unit was established.

children.label: A
children.text: be subject to valid existing rights, including any right of the United States to income derived, directly or indirectly, from a lease, license, permit, right-of-way or easement; and

children.label: B
children.text: reserve to the United States deposits of oil, gas and coal, together with the right to explore, mine, and remove these minerals, on lands which the Secretary determines to be prospectively valuable for development.

children.label: A
children.text: would have been eligible for an allotment under the Act of (chapter 2469; ), as that Act was in effect before (except that the term “nonmineral”, as used in that Act, shall for the purpose of this subsection be defined as provided in , except that such definition shall not apply to land within a conservation system unit); andMay 17, 1906December 18, 197134 Stat. 197section 1634(a)(3) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: served at least 6 months between and ; orJanuary 1, 1969December 31, 1971

children.label: ii
children.text: enlisted or was drafted into military service after but before .June 2, 1971December 3, 1971

children.label: i
children.text: was killed in action;

children.label: ii
children.text: was wounded in action and subsequently died as a direct consequence of that wound, as determined by the Department of Veterans Affairs or based on other evidence acceptable to the Secretary; or

children.label: iii
children.text: died while a prisoner of war.

children.label: I
children.text: provide a determination to the Secretary if the records of the Department of Veterans Affairs contain sufficient information to support such a determination; or

children.label: II
children.text: notify the Secretary that the records of the Department of Veterans Affairs do not contain sufficient information to support a determination and that further investigation will be necessary.

children.label: ii
children.text: Not later than 1 year after notification to the Secretary that further investigation is necessary, the Department of Veterans Affairs shall complete the investigation and provide a determination to the Secretary.
No person who received an allotment or has a pending allotment under the Act of may receive an allotment under this section.May 17, 1906

children.label: A
children.text: served during a period other than that specified in subsection (b)(1)(B);

children.label: B
children.text: were eligible for an allotment under the Act of ; andMay 17, 1906

children.label: C
children.text: did not apply for an allotment under that Act.

The Secretary shall, within one year of , issue a written report on the study, including findings and recommendations, to the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources in the Senate and the Committee on Appropriations and the Committee on Resources in the House of Representatives.October 21, 1998

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: d Definitions
children.text: For the purposes of this section, the terms “veteran” and “Vietnam era” have the meanings given those terms by paragraphs (2) and (29), respectively, of .section 101 of title 38

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: e Regulations
children.text: No later than 18 months after , the Secretary of the Interior shall promulgate, after consultation with Alaska Natives groups, rules to carry out this section.October 21, 1998
